title: Source Fact (Provenance Record)
description: v1 canonical provenance record for a single fact used by the platform. Every PRD section 9 mandatory provenance field is REQUIRED here (fix for M0-T004 G3 defect D1): source id, original field name, original value, normalized value, retrieved timestamp, dataset version, effective date (nullable but the key must be present), BBL applicability, confidence, user confirmed/overridden, conflict status. A material calculation may never be exported without a resolvable record of this shape (PRD section 19). TASK M2-T004 (additive, property-profile contract 1.2.0): four OPTIONAL identity/lineage keys - stable fact_key (survives re-observation), immutable observation_id (unique per retrieval event), value_digest and response_digest (canonical-json-1 digests, see common.schema.json digest_sha256). The required v1 field set is unchanged, so every pre-M2-T004 record remains valid; the backend emits all four on every new fact so each fact traces observation -> source version (dataset_version) -> request (request_url + retrieved_at) without gaps. TASK M2-T017 (DF-4, whole-system trust replan Area G): this record is now CLOSED with additionalProperties:false so a mandatory provenance record can no longer SILENTLY accept an undocumented or mistyped key (a typo of an OPTIONAL field, or a leaked diagnostic/internal field, is rejected instead of passing through). No conformant record is rejected: the four connector-emitted lineage keys that accepted connectors already produce (dataset_id, request_url, input_vintages [PLUTO], source_rows_updated_at [ZTLDB]) are now DOCUMENTED optional properties rather than open-schema extras, so the closed contract exactly matches real producer output. The $id/path stay v1 (the property_profile contract $refs this file by name and is out of this task's scope); this is an in-place hardening of the v1 contract, additive in documented keys and restrictive only against genuinely-undocumented keys.

provenance_id.description: Stable identifier of this provenance record. Referenced by property-profile fact values via provenance_ref.

source_id.description: Source-registry identifier of the official source (PRD section 8.2). Synthetic/test sources must use a clearly non-official id (e.g. prefixed 'test-').

original_field_name.description: Field name exactly as exposed by the source, e.g. Geoclient 'bblTaxBlock' or GeoSearch 'properties.addendum.pad.bbl' (docs/research/M0-T002-geoclient-address-resolution.md sections 2.6 and 3).

original_value.description: Verbatim value as returned by the source, unconverted and untrimmed (Geosupport values may intentionally retain significant whitespace, e.g. censusTract ' 187 '; research doc section 2.5). Any JSON type.

normalized_value.description: Platform-normalized value derived deterministically from original_value. Any JSON type. Normalization code, never AI, produces this value.

units.description: Units of the normalized value where applicable (PRD section 19 evidence viewer); null when unitless. Optional key.

retrieved_at.description: Timestamp at which the value was retrieved from the source (PRD section 9).

dataset_version.description: Dataset version or page/document version (PRD section 9). Examples grounded in research: Geosupport/PAD quarterly release id such as '26B' (DCP GDE page), GeoSearch 'addendum.pad.version' such as '26a' (docs/research/M0-T002-geoclient-address-resolution.md sections 3-5).

effective_date.description: Effective date where the source publishes one (PRD section 9 'where available'). The key is REQUIRED and explicitly null when the source publishes none, so absence is a visible statement rather than a silent omission.

bbl.description: Property/BBL to which this fact applies (PRD section 9).

confidence.description: Confidence in the normalized fact, 0-1 (e.g. GeoSearch match 'confidence': 0.8, research doc section 3; deterministic official-record retrievals use 1). PRD section 12: confidence scores must never substitute for legal-review/coverage status.

user_confirmed_or_overridden.description: Whether the user confirmed or overrode this fact (PRD section 9). PLATFORM-DEFINED enum (not a government-source value): grounded in PRD sections 5 (confirm/correct flow) and 9; 'none' = not yet reviewed by the user.

conflict_status.description: Conflict status of this fact across sources (PRD section 9). PLATFORM-DEFINED enum (not a government-source value): grounded in PRD sections 2/9 (conflicts must remain visible); 'resolved' requires a matching resolution entry in the property profile's conflicts list.

fact_key.description: TASK M2-T004, OPTIONAL (additive; owner P1 bullet 2): STABLE identity of the LOGICAL fact, independent of when it was retrieved. Re-observing the same fact (same source, dataset, property, source field) at any later time - including under a new dataset version - yields the SAME fact_key, letting persistence and diffing track a fact's value across releases. Construction is connector-specific and documented per connector; the PLUTO connector uses 'fact:<source_id>:<dataset_id>:<bbl>:<original_field_name>'. Distinct from provenance_id (stable within one dataset version) and observation_id (unique per retrieval event). OPTIONAL so that pre-M2-T004 records remain valid; the backend emits it on every new fact.

observation_id.description: TASK M2-T004, OPTIONAL (additive; owner P1 bullet 2): IMMUTABLE identity of ONE observation of the fact - unique per retrieval event, never reused, never reassigned. Two retrievals of byte-identical content still get different observation_ids (the digests, not the observation_id, express content equality). The PLUTO connector uses 'obs:<retrieval-event-id>:<bbl>:<original_field_name>' where the retrieval-event id is minted fresh for every fetch, so all facts of one fetch share the event segment. Together with fact_key this separates 'which fact' from 'which retrieval' (snapshot lineage: observation -> source version via dataset_version -> request via request_url/retrieved_at).

value_digest.description: TASK M2-T004, OPTIONAL (additive; owner P1 bullet 3): canonical digest of this fact's verbatim original_value (the PARSED source value, canonical-json-1 form - see common.schema.json digest_sha256). Deterministic: identical source values digest equal across retrievals; any value change flips the digest.

response_digest.description: TASK M2-T004, OPTIONAL (additive; owner P1 bullets 3-4): canonical digest of the ENTIRE parsed source response body this observation came from (canonical-json-1 form). All facts of one retrieval event share it, tying every fact to the exact response snapshot; equals reproducibility.response_digest in the property profile built from the same retrieval.

dataset_id.description: TASK M2-T017, OPTIONAL (additive; DOCUMENTING an existing connector-emitted lineage key so the record can be CLOSED rather than left open): the source's dataset identifier. The Socrata connectors emit their dataset's 4x4 resource token here (PLUTO 'DATASET_ID=64uk-42ks', ZTLDB 'DATASET_ID=fdkv-4t4z'; services/api/app/connectors/pluto_soda.py, ztldb_soda.py). It is the machine identity of the dataset behind dataset_version and appears verbatim in the deterministic provenance_id. OPTIONAL so pre-M2-T017 records and connectors that do not carry a stable dataset token (e.g. the wave/ArcGIS provenance records) stay valid.

request_url.description: TASK M2-T017, OPTIONAL (additive; DOCUMENTING an existing connector-emitted lineage key): the exact official request URL this observation was retrieved from, so the retrieval can be repeated against the same endpoint (PRD section 9 provenance; the Socrata connectors set it - pluto_soda.py, ztldb_soda.py). Secrets/tokens are NEVER part of this URL - they travel in request headers (mirrors property_profile.reproducibility.request_url). Equals reproducibility.request_url in the profile built from the same retrieval. OPTIONAL so pre-M2-T017 records stay valid.

input_vintages.description: TASK M2-T017, OPTIONAL (additive; DOCUMENTING an existing PLUTO-connector-emitted lineage key): a map of the source's input-vintage date columns actually present in the retrieved record (services/api/app/connectors/pluto_soda.py VINTAGE_DATE_COLUMNS - basempdate, dcasdate, edesigdate, landmkdate, masdate, polidate, rpaddate, zoningdate, ...) to their VERBATIM source values (any JSON type). These are the official per-input 'DATES OF DATA' effective-date bounds the source publishes at the dataset level, distinct from the per-fact effective_date (which stays null when the source publishes no per-column effective date). Empty {} when the record exposes none. The inner map is intentionally open (source-driven column names are never guessed into a closed set); the TOP-LEVEL record is closed. OPTIONAL and PLUTO-specific, so records from other connectors stay valid.

source_rows_updated_at.description: TASK M2-T017, OPTIONAL (additive; DOCUMENTING an existing ZTLDB-connector-emitted lineage key): the official dataset 'rows last updated' timestamp (RFC 3339) the ZTLDB SODA connector uses as its dataset-version freshness signal (services/api/app/connectors/ztldb_soda.py; ZTLDB publishes no per-record release token, so rowsUpdatedAt is the version signal that becomes dataset_version). This exposes source-publication freshness WITHOUT ever marking a fresh transport as cached/stale (the two-staleness rule). OPTIONAL and ZTLDB-specific, so records from other connectors stay valid.
